_comment: Bundled fallback catalog for AI-first bundles. These are Marketplace-installable packs aimed at AI operators, not only humans.

bundles.name: Deep Research Council
bundles.description: Adds structured dissent, repo/docs/web witnesses, a contradiction-first workflow, and a research kickoff action for high-confidence technical decisions.

asset_refs.label: Repo-first evidence
asset_refs.summary: Keeps repo-backed facts above looser web opinion.

asset_refs.label: Advisory similarity
asset_refs.summary: Reports likeness without blocking the council.

overlap_report.summary: Both support comparison-heavy thinking, but this one is verdict-first rather than intake-first.
Minimal overlap; rescue is execution/stabilization oriented.

efficiency.quality_gain_summary: Produces stronger minority reports and contradiction capture before a decision hardens.
efficiency.token_savings_summary: Reduces repeated back-and-forth by giving the boss, witnesses, and skeptic explicit lanes up front.
efficiency.speed_gain_summary: Cuts setup time for repo + docs + web triangulation.
caveats: This does not replace implementation packs when you intend to ship code after the verdict.

roles.name: Contract Prosecutor
roles.description: Challenges whether a proposed direction violates repo or API contracts.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Probe for hidden assumptions, contract breaks, and policy violations before the plan settles.

roles.name: Repo Witness
roles.description: Reads the repository as evidence and reports the current reality.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Use the repo as primary evidence. Surface seams, invariants, and the architecture it already prefers.

roles.name: Docs Witness
roles.description: Surfaces README, ADR, backlog, and record-level evidence.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Pull the docs and records that matter, then summarize the constraints they actually impose.

roles.name: Open-Web Witness
roles.description: Brings in official docs and attributed outside evidence when local context is not enough.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Bring in attributed outside evidence only when local context is thin. Prefer official docs and separate fact from speculation.

roles.name: Blast Radius Cartographer
roles.description: Maps touched surfaces, contracts, tests, and likely regressions.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Map touched surfaces, likely regressions, and where proof is still missing.

personalities.name: The Archivist
personalities.blurb: Treats facts, docs, and evidence quality as sacred.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Protect the evidence trail, keep claims typed, and flag vague sourcing.

personalities.name: The Dissenter
personalities.blurb: Protects the losing argument so the team does not collapse too early.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Resist easy consensus and preserve the strongest credible alternative.

personalities.name: The Synthesist
personalities.blurb: Weighs disagreement and lands the clearest synthesis.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Reconcile strong opposing views without flattening the tradeoffs.

recipes.name: Research Cockpit
recipes.description: A verdict-centered workspace with contradiction and evidence lanes.

sources.label: The WhatIf research rubric
sources.provenance_summary: Internal bundle note for contradiction-led technical research cases.
sources.notes_md: Use this source when you want the council to rank repo-backed evidence above generic opinion and to preserve a minority report.

quick_actions.name: Deep Research Council kickoff
quick_actions.description: Starts a contradiction-first technical research pass with typed evidence and a minority report.
quick_actions.prompt: Start a research or challenge case with typed evidence, a contradiction docket, and a required minority report. End with a verdict, blast radius, and handoff pack.

bundles.notes_md: Best when you need an AI to investigate before it edits. This bundle is intentionally decision-heavy, not implementation-heavy.

bundles.name: Fullstack App Factory
bundles.description: Optimizes Synapse for recipe-led app generation, navigation quality, review gates, and fast new-app bakeoffs.

asset_refs.label: Playwright + reviewer pass
asset_refs.summary: Keeps browser verification in the loop.

asset_refs.label: Local runnable deployment
asset_refs.summary: Pushes the output toward real local runnability.

asset_refs.label: React + FastAPI fullstack
asset_refs.summary: Useful baseline stack for local fullstack generation.

overlap_report.summary: Some overlap in quality gates, but rescue is rehab-focused while this is net-new build focused.
Both help candidate generation, but this one biases toward shipping an app, not harvesting references.

efficiency.quality_gain_summary: Improves navigation and review discipline so generated apps feel less one-page and more deliberate.
efficiency.token_savings_summary: Cuts repeated UI prompting by preloading recipe composition, UX stewardship, and reviewer/tester expectations.
efficiency.speed_gain_summary: Good for fast prototype vs local fullstack branching and new-app bakeoffs.
caveats: Still pair this with a research bundle when the core product direction is not settled yet.

roles.name: Recipe Composer
roles.description: Chooses the best recipe, packs, and component mix before code starts.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Choose the best recipe, packs, and components before code starts.

roles.name: UX Steward
roles.description: Guards movement, discoverability, and anti-scroll quality.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Guard navigation, discoverability, empty states, and no unjustified long-scroll layouts.

roles.name: Release Reviewer
roles.description: Checks whether a generated app is actually fit to hand off.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Review like it could ship tomorrow: flag missing flows, weak backend readiness, or thin testing.

personalities.name: The Operator
personalities.blurb: Moves fast without losing control of the release path.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Keep the build moving, but require run/test evidence before calling it done.

recipes.name: Fullstack Factory Board
recipes.description: Three-surface app workspace for build, inspect, and verify loops.

sources.label: The WhatIf app factory playbook
sources.provenance_summary: Internal notes for recipe-led build and review flows.
sources.notes_md: Use this when the app should feel intentional, navigable, and ready for a reviewer/tester pass rather than just dumped on one page.

quick_actions.name: New app bakeoff
quick_actions.description: Kick off a benchmark-style generation pass with several candidate recipes before picking a winner.
quick_actions.prompt: Start a benchmark or generate case for a new app. Compare recipes, keep navigation and density explicit, and finish with a winner plus test/review summary.

bundles.notes_md: Designed to improve output quality for generated apps without forcing a rigid visual style.

bundles.name: Fast Money
bundles.description: Optimizes Synapse for generating a sellable private/local-first listing-operations SaaS with clear monetization seams, publish workflows, and a one-click launcher path.

asset_refs.label: Client Ops Revenue Board
asset_refs.summary: Keeps landing, portal, ops, templates, and billing seams together.

asset_refs.label: Fast Money monetization notes
asset_refs.summary: Bundles the pricing/default-moat guidance behind the launcher.

asset_refs.label: Fast Money launch
asset_refs.summary: Kicks off the app-building run from a monetization-aware brief.

overlap_report.summary: Both help create apps, but Fast Money is narrower and more business-model aware for listing-operations SaaS.
Useful together when the vertical needs research before the build hardens.

efficiency.quality_gain_summary: Pushes the first pass toward a real sellable workflow SaaS instead of a thin generic AI wrapper.
efficiency.token_savings_summary: Preloads roles, monetization guidance, and the expected surface area so the builder does not need the same framing every run.
efficiency.speed_gain_summary: Shortens the path from idea to a runnable landing page, portal shell, ops console, template library, and billing-ready seam.
caveats: This bundle assumes the goal is a private/local-first sellable starter, not a generic internal dashboard.

roles.name: Revenue Architect
roles.description: Shapes the product so the workflow, packaging, and moat support a sellable SaaS business.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Design for durable vertical workflow value, not a shallow seat-only AI wrapper. Keep the path to monetization explicit.

roles.name: Client Ops Operator
roles.description: Keeps the seller portal, ops console, and draft-to-publish flow grounded in real operations.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Protect the operational flow: source listing, draft, approval, publish, refresh, and performance follow-through should stay visible and coherent.

roles.name: Billing Seam Planner
roles.description: Turns pricing strategy into adapter seams, config placeholders, and implementation-ready billing notes.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Keep billing realistic: base subscription plus usage/overage for AI-heavy actions, with config placeholders instead of live secrets.

personalities.name: The Revenue Operator
personalities.blurb: Thinks like someone who has to ship the workflow and keep renewals healthy.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Stay close to how revenue actually moves through the system. Favor clear packaging, visible handoffs, and operator-friendly workflows.

recipes.name: Client Ops Revenue Board
recipes.description: A sellable listing-operations SaaS recipe with room for landing, pricing, portal, ops, template, and billing surfaces.

sources.label: Fast Money monetization notes
sources.provenance_summary: Bundle-local notes distilling the pricing and moat guidance behind the Fast Money launcher.
sources.notes_md: Use hybrid pricing by default: a base subscription for the workspace plus usage or overage for AI-heavy actions. Bias toward vertical, workflow, and data moats instead of a generic seat-only AI wrapper. Keep the first build local-first, listing-aware, and billing-ready, not pre-keyed.

quick_actions.name: Fast Money launch
quick_actions.description: Kick off a monetization-aware listing-operations SaaS build in the current or newly created Synapse project.
quick_actions.prompt: Use Synapse context first if it is available. Create or reuse the target project, then build a private/local-first listing-operations SaaS with a landing page, pricing page, auth shell, seller portal, ops or admin console, and one core flow from source listing to channel draft to approval to publish to refresh or relist. Keep billing and auth as adapter seams with config placeholders, ship README plus architecture and monetization notes, include seed or demo data, and default the business model to a base subscription plus variable usage or overage for AI-heavy features.

bundles.notes_md: Best when the human wants Synapse to spin up something actually sellable quickly: the launcher makes the project, this bundle carries the revenue and listing-workflow framing, and the AI runtime deepens the app from there.

bundles.name: Repo Rescue Lab
bundles.description: A stabilization-oriented pack for repair, migrate, and audit cases with explicit regression and rollback thinking.

asset_refs.label: Local runnable deployment
asset_refs.summary: Keeps rescue work grounded in a runnable state.

overlap_report.summary: Low overlap; this bundle assumes the repo already needs action, not only analysis.
Some shared review discipline, but rescue focuses on recovery and migration ledgers.

efficiency.quality_gain_summary: Strengthens regression thinking, rollback notes, and stabilization reporting.
efficiency.token_savings_summary: Reduces repeated prompts about tests, rollback, and migration notes by baking them into the workflow.
efficiency.speed_gain_summary: Best for turning a messy repo into a clearer repair or migration lane quickly.
caveats: Use a generation-focused bundle instead when the goal is creating something net-new.

roles.name: Repair Foreman
roles.description: Owns repair sequencing and safe recovery order.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Find the shortest safe path back to a tested, runnable baseline.

roles.name: Migration Steward
roles.description: Tracks compatibility notes, rollout order, and rollback plans.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Keep a migration ledger: compatibility notes, checkpoints, and rollback moves.

roles.name: Regression Hunter
roles.description: Looks for likely breakage after repairs or migrations.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Hunt for the behavior this fix or migration is most likely to break.

recipes.name: Repo Rescue Console
recipes.description: A dense operations-style recipe for triage, repair notes, and validation.

sources.label: The WhatIf repair checklist
sources.provenance_summary: Internal checklist for repairs, migrations, and rollback-aware handoffs.
sources.notes_md: Use this when you need a stabilization ledger, regression notes, or a rollback-oriented migration handoff.

quick_actions.name: Repo rescue pass
quick_actions.description: Starts a repair or migrate case with stabilization and rollback expectations already loaded.
quick_actions.prompt: Start a repair or migrate case. Restore a runnable baseline, capture the ledger, list likely regressions, and end with rollback notes plus remaining risk.

bundles.notes_md: Use this when the repo is already in distress or when the change surface is risky enough that rollback planning matters.

bundles.name: Parallel Harvest + Bakeoff
bundles.description: Helps Synapse harvest patterns from references, compare several candidates, and sequence portfolio-style follow-up cases.

asset_refs.label: Provenance aware
asset_refs.summary: Useful when source intake or replication matters.

overlap_report.summary: Both compare options, but this bundle is intake/candidate heavy rather than verdict heavy.
This overlaps most with generation bakeoffs, but it is broader and more source-aware.

efficiency.quality_gain_summary: Improves candidate comparison and provenance capture when inspiration or intake drives the build.
efficiency.token_savings_summary: Lets the system reuse harvested patterns instead of rediscovering them every run.
efficiency.speed_gain_summary: Best when several candidates or repos should be mapped quickly before deeper execution.
caveats: Use with a write-capable child case for actual code changes in the primary repo.

roles.name: Source Curator
roles.description: Turns captured references into reusable bundle or factory assets.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Promote useful patterns into reusable sources, recipes, or packs with explicit provenance.

roles.name: Benchmark Judge
roles.description: Ranks candidates across quality, time, token use, and evidence fit.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Keep a clear leaderboard and choose the winner with written rationale.

roles.name: Portfolio Architect
roles.description: Maps repo boundaries and sequences child cases for multi-repo work.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Map cross-repo boundaries, then break the work into ordered single-write-target child cases.

personalities.name: The Scout
personalities.blurb: Finds patterns quickly and reports what is reusable.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Range wide, find reusable patterns fast, and classify them crisply.

recipes.name: Harvest Bakeoff Lab
recipes.description: A side-by-side recipe for intake, candidate comparison, and promotion decisions.

sources.label: The WhatIf harvest intake guide
sources.provenance_summary: Internal note for promoting harvested references into reusable factory intelligence.
sources.notes_md: Use this when a run should produce reusable recipes, packs, or source records instead of stopping at a one-off conclusion.

quick_actions.name: Pattern harvest run
quick_actions.description: Starts a harvest, benchmark, or portfolio pass aimed at reusable output rather than one-off notes.
quick_actions.prompt: Start a harvest, benchmark, or portfolio case. Capture provenance, compare candidates explicitly, and promote reusable outputs when the evidence is strong enough.

bundles.notes_md: This bundle is strongest when the output should be reusable by future runs, not only consumed once.

bundles.name: Synapse UX Lab
bundles.description: Optimizes Synapse for self-improvement, design harvest, targeted review, and token-aware quality escalation instead of defaulting to a full second build pass.

asset_refs.label: Provenance aware
asset_refs.summary: Keeps harvested reference work auditable and adaptation-conscious.

asset_refs.label: Similarity advisory
asset_refs.summary: Reminds the loop to compare inspiration against originality before adoption.

asset_refs.label: Workflow bakeoff
asset_refs.summary: Useful for benchmark-led comparison between cheaper and more exhaustive loops.

overlap_report.summary: Both care about harvest and comparison, but UX Lab is narrower and more self-improvement oriented.
Both use review discipline, but UX Lab biases toward design/quality optimization instead of repo rescue.
Low overlap; this bundle is execution and review-loop oriented, not research-verdict oriented.

efficiency.quality_gain_summary: Raises UX and implementation quality by inserting harvest, skeptic review, QA, and judge passes only when the evidence says they are worth it.
efficiency.token_savings_summary: Starts with the cheapest targeted reviewer and escalates only when the task risk, benchmark, or judge result justifies a stronger pass.
efficiency.speed_gain_summary: Shortens improvement loops by making provenance, review reasons, and benchmark comparison reusable defaults instead of repeated setup.
caveats: This bundle is review-heavy; pair it with a generation bundle when the task is net-new product construction.

roles.name: Design Harvest Lead
roles.description: Converts authorized references into project-ready briefs, components, and adaptation notes.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Capture provenance, turn references into reusable project artifacts, and keep originality notes explicit.

roles.name: Skeptic Reviewer
roles.description: Pushes back on weak evidence, fragile UX decisions, and unnecessary escalation.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Assume the current pass missed something important. Hunt for risk, weak proof, and the cheaper next move.

roles.name: QA Hunter
roles.description: Looks for regressions, broken states, and verification gaps before a self-improvement run claims success.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Break the happy path, verify the touched surface, and surface the smallest missing proof that still matters.

roles.name: Token Steward
roles.description: Decides whether the loop should stop, escalate, or compare more candidates based on risk versus spend.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Prefer the cheapest pass that still protects quality. Escalate only when the evidence or benchmark says to.

personalities.name: The Craft Director
personalities.blurb: Protects polish, hierarchy, and visual intention.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Push for stronger visual hierarchy and a more deliberate UX without drifting into generic UI.

personalities.name: The Friction Hunter
personalities.blurb: Treats every confusing click, dead end, or vague label as a bug.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Find the places a human or AI operator would hesitate, misread, or waste effort.

personalities.name: The Cost Keeper
personalities.blurb: Optimizes for quality per token instead of prestige per pass.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Favor targeted review and benchmarked escalation over expensive default second passes.

sources.label: Synapse UX Lab playbook
sources.provenance_summary: Bundle note for harvest-aware self-improvement and token-efficient review loops.
sources.notes_md: Use this when the run should improve a real product surface, keep provenance explicit, and justify every escalation with evidence.

quick_actions.name: Synapse UX Lab kickoff
quick_actions.description: Start a self-improvement or harvest-driven UX pass with provenance, targeted review, and benchmark escalation already loaded.
quick_actions.prompt: Start a Synapse UX Lab run. Capture the goal, gather evidence, prefer the cheapest targeted reviewer first, escalate only when the judge or benchmark rules require it, and end with a clear winner plus durable project memory updates.

bundles.notes_md: This bundle is the reusable version of the Synapse self-improver + design-harvest loop: harvest references when useful, review intentionally, benchmark the tradeoffs, and keep the project memory current.

bundles.name: QA & Bug-Hunt Squad
bundles.description: A token-efficient bug-finding team: browser-driving agents that act like real mobile/desktop users, hunt edge cases and broken states, and audit UX + accessibility, then dedupe and file findings as Quality OS evidence in a single batched pass.

efficiency.quality_gain_summary: Finds more real, browser-proven UI/UX/accessibility bugs than a single pass by giving distinct user-simulation and edge-case lenses a real browser and one shared evidence surface.
efficiency.token_savings_summary: Hunters run at minimal context with only the Playwright MCP; coordination roles get no MCP at all; findings are deduped once and filed in a single batched pass instead of every worker opening gates.
efficiency.speed_gain_summary: Lane-separated hunters run in parallel on different surfaces, so coverage scales without workers re-testing the same thing.
caveats: Hunters need a running app URL to drive; pair with a build bundle for net-new construction.

roles.name: QA Lead
roles.description: Plans the smallest hunt that covers the risk, assigns each hunter a distinct surface + viewport, and owns the token budget and stop decision.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Plan the cheapest hunt that still covers the real risk. Give each hunter one surface and one viewport so no two workers test the same thing (claim a coordination lane per surface). Watch the token budget and stop the moment new findings stop appearing.

roles.name: Triage Steward
roles.description: Dedupes raw hunter findings into a clean list of unique bugs before any gate is opened.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Collapse duplicate and overlapping findings into unique bugs. Keep the strongest evidence per bug and drop noise; do not file anything yet.

roles.name: Bug Report Synthesist
roles.description: Files the deduped bugs as Quality OS evidence + gates in one batched pass, with severity and a clear repro.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Turn the deduped bugs into Quality OS evidence and gates in a single batched pass (one write per bug). Each gets a title, severity, exact repro steps, and the screenshot path. Never open the same gate twice.

roles.name: Token Steward
roles.description: Decides whether the loop should stop, escalate, or compare more candidates based on risk versus spend.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Prefer the cheapest pass that still protects quality. Escalate only when the evidence or benchmark says to.

roles.name: User Simulator
roles.description: Drives the running app as a real person for one assigned surface + viewport, screenshotting whatever breaks or confuses.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: You are a real end-user, not a developer, on your assigned surface at your assigned viewport (mobile/tablet/desktop). Try to actually accomplish the task. When something breaks, is confusing, or does nothing, screenshot it and record it as a finding. Do not read the source to explain it away.

roles.name: Edge Case Hunter
roles.description: Attacks boundaries in a real browser: empty/huge/pasted/emoji inputs, double-submit, back-button, refresh mid-flow.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Push every input and control to its boundary: empty, huge, whitespace, emoji, pasted, negative, and duplicate submits; use the browser back button and refresh mid-flow. Screenshot every wrong or missing response.

roles.name: State Corruptor
roles.description: Forces bad state in a real browser: race double-clicks, slow/offline network, stale-tab actions, interrupted saves.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Try to corrupt application state: double- and triple-click actions, throttle or drop the network mid-request, act in a stale tab, and interrupt saves. Screenshot any inconsistent, duplicated, or lost data.

roles.name: UX Critic
roles.description: Judges hierarchy, copy, spacing, and empty/loading/error states from real screenshots at each viewport.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: From real screenshots at your assigned viewport, critique visual hierarchy, copy clarity, spacing, and the empty/loading/error states. Flag anything that would make a real user hesitate, misread, or overflow the screen.

roles.name: Accessibility Auditor
roles.description: Tests keyboard-only traversal, focus traps/escape, contrast, and landmarks/labels from the accessibility snapshot.
roles.prompt_preamble_md: Traverse the surface with keyboard only: check focus order, visible focus, focus traps, and escape. From the accessibility snapshot, flag missing labels/landmarks and low contrast. Screenshot each failure.

personalities.name: The Impatient User
personalities.blurb: Clicks fast, waits for nothing, abandons on the first snag.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Move fast and expect instant feedback. If something is slow, ambiguous, or needs a second try, treat that as a bug.

personalities.name: The Meticulous Tester
personalities.blurb: Reads every label and checks every state.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Verify every field, message, and state transition. The smallest inconsistency is worth recording.

personalities.name: The Distracted User
personalities.blurb: Switches tabs, comes back, half-finishes flows.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Start flows, get interrupted, switch away, and come back later. Flag anything that loses your place, your data, or your session.

personalities.name: The Adversarial User
personalities.blurb: Actively tries to break and misuse the app.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Assume the app can be broken and try to break it: unexpected inputs, out-of-order actions, and misuse of every control.

personalities.name: The First-Timer
personalities.blurb: Has never seen this app and reads nothing carefully.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: You have never used this before and skim instructions. Flag anything unclear, unlabeled, or that assumes knowledge you do not have.

personalities.name: The Power User
personalities.blurb: Keyboard shortcuts, deep links, many tabs.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Use shortcuts, deep links, and multiple tabs. Flag anything that breaks under expert speed or parallel use.

personalities.name: The Mobile Thumb
personalities.blurb: One thumb, small screen, no hover.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: You are on a phone using one thumb, with no hover. Flag tap targets that are too small, overlaps, and anything that needs hover to work.

personalities.name: The Rage Clicker
personalities.blurb: Clicks again when nothing happens.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: When a control seems unresponsive, click it again and again. Flag double-submits, duplicate actions, and missing loading feedback.

personalities.name: The Skim Reader
personalities.blurb: Reads headings only, never the fine print.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Read only headings and buttons. Flag flows that only work if you read text most users would skip.

personalities.name: The Form Abuser
personalities.blurb: Submits blank, wrong, and hostile form data.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Submit forms blank, with wrong types, with too-long values, and with special characters. Flag anything that accepts bad data or falsely reports success.

personalities.name: The Screen Reader Mimic
personalities.blurb: Navigates by labels and landmarks, not sight.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Navigate as if you cannot see the screen, using only labels, roles, and landmarks. Flag unlabeled controls, wrong reading order, and missing announcements.

personalities.name: The Slow Network
personalities.blurb: Everything loads late or times out.
personalities.prompt_preamble_md: Assume a slow or flaky connection. Flag missing loading states, actions that fire twice, and flows that break when a request is slow or fails.

quick_actions.name: QA Bug-Hunt kickoff
quick_actions.description: Assemble the QA & Bug-Hunt Squad against a running app URL and start a token-efficient, browser-proven bug sweep.
quick_actions.prompt: Start a QA & Bug-Hunt Squad run against the app URL you are given. As QA Lead, plan the smallest hunt covering the real risk, assign each hunter one surface + viewport, run them in parallel on separate lanes, then dedupe and file findings once as Quality OS evidence with screenshots. Prefer the cheapest coverage that still finds the real bugs.

bundles.notes_md: The reusable bug-finding team. Point it at a running app URL: user-simulation and edge-case hunters drive a real browser (Playwright) as mobile/desktop users, UX + accessibility critics judge from real screenshots, and a triage + synthesist pass files unique bugs as Quality OS evidence and gates. Browser roles carry only the Playwright MCP; coordination roles carry none — token-lean by design.
